name: poetry

metadata.name: poetry
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: Python dependency management and packaging made easy.

documentation:
# Poetry: Python packaging and dependency management made easy

[![Stable Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/poetry?label=stable)][PyPI Releases]
[![Pre-release Version](https://img.shields.io/github/v/release/python-poetry/poetry?label=pre-release&include_prereleases&sort=semver)][PyPI Releases]
[![Python Versions](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/poetry)][PyPI]
[![Download Stats](https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/poetry)](https://pypistats.org/packages/poetry)
[![Discord](https://img.shields.io/discord/487711540787675139?logo=discord)][Discord]

Poetry helps you declare, manage and install dependencies of Python projects,
ensuring you have the right stack everywhere.

![Poetry Install](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/python-poetry/poetry/master/assets/install.gif)

Poetry replaces `setup.py`, `requirements.txt`, `setup.cfg`, `MANIFEST.in` and `Pipfile` with a simple `pyproject.toml`
based project format.

```toml
[tool.poetry]
name = "my-package"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "The description of the package"

license = "MIT"

authors = [
    "Sébastien Eustace <sebastien@eustace.io>"
]

repository = "https://github.com/python-poetry/poetry"
homepage = "https://python-poetry.org"

# README file(s) are used as the package description
readme = ["README.md", "LICENSE"]

# Keywords (translated to tags on the package index)
keywords = ["packaging", "poetry"]

[tool.poetry.dependencies]
# Compatible Python versions
python = ">=3.8"
# Standard dependency with semver constraints
aiohttp = "^3.8.1"
# Dependency with extras
requests = { version = "^2.28", extras = ["security"] }
# Version-specific dependencies with prereleases allowed
tomli = { version = "^2.0.1", python = "<3.11", allow-prereleases = true }
# Git dependencies
cleo = { git = "https://github.com/python-poetry/cleo.git", branch = "master" }
# Optional dependencies (installed by extras)
pendulum = { version = "^2.1.2", optional = true }

# Dependency groups are supported for organizing your dependencies
[tool.poetry.group.dev.dependencies]
pytest = "^7.1.2"
pytest-cov = "^3.0"

# ...and can be installed only when explicitly requested
[tool.poetry.group.docs]
optional = true
[tool.poetry.group.docs.dependencies]
Sphinx = "^5.1.1"

# Python-style entrypoints and scripts are easily expressed
[tool.poetry.scripts]
my-script = "my_package:main"
```

## Installation

Poetry supports multiple installation methods, including a simple script found at [install.python-poetry.org]. For full
installation instructions, including advanced usage of the script, alternate install methods, and CI best practices, see
the full [installation documentation].

## Documentation

[Documentation] for the current version of Poetry (as well as the development branch and recently out of support
versions) is available from the [official website].

## Contribute

Poetry is a large, complex project always in need of contributors. For those new to the project, a list of
[suggested issues] to work on in Poetry and poetry-core is available. The full [contributing documentation] also
provides helpful guidance.

## Resources

* [Releases][PyPI Releases]
* [Official Website]
* [Documentation]
* [Issue Tracker]
* [Discord]

  [PyPI]: https://pypi.org/project/poetry/
  [PyPI Releases]: https://pypi.org/project/poetry/#history
  [Official Website]: https://python-poetry.org
  [Documentation]: https://python-poetry.org/docs/
  [Issue Tracker]: https://github.com/python-poetry/poetry/issues
  [Suggested Issues]: https://github.com/python-poetry/poetry/contribute
  [Contributing Documentation]: https://python-poetry.org/docs/contributing
  [Discord]: https://discord.com/invite/awxPgve
  [install.python-poetry.org]: https://install.python-poetry.org
  [Installation Documentation]: https://python-poetry.org/docs/#installation

## Related Projects

* [poetry-core](https://github.com/python-poetry/poetry-core): PEP 517 build-system for Poetry projects, and
dependency-free core functionality of the Poetry frontend
* [poetry-plugin-export](https://github.com/python-poetry/poetry-plugin-export): Export Poetry projects/lock files to
foreign formats like requirements.txt
* [poetry-plugin-bundle](https://github.com/python-poetry/poetry-plugin-bundle): Install Poetry projects/lock files to
external formats like virtual environments
* [install.python-poetry.org](https://github.com/python-poetry/install.python-poetry.org): The official Poetry
installation script
* [website](https://github.com/python-poetry/website): The official Poetry website and blog

methods.name: poetry.config.config.Config.get
documentation: Retrieve a setting value.

methods.name: poetry.console.commands.group_command.GroupCommand.default_group
documentation:
The default group to use when no group is specified. This is useful
for command that have the `--group` option, eg: add, remove.

Can be overridden to adapt behavior.

methods.name: poetry.console.commands.group_command.GroupCommand.default_groups
documentation:
The groups that are considered by the command by default.

Can be overridden to adapt behavior.

methods.name: poetry.console.commands.self.self_command.SelfCommand._system_project_handle
documentation:
This is a helper method that by default calls the handle method implemented in
the child class's next MRO sibling. Override this if you want special handling
either before calling the handle() from the super class or have custom logic
to handle the command.

The default implementations handles cases where a `self` command delegates
handling to an existing command. Eg: `SelfAddCommand(SelfCommand, AddCommand)`.

methods.name: poetry.console.commands.self.self_command.SelfCommand.reset
documentation:
Reset current command instance's environment and poetry instances to ensure
use of the system specific ones.

classes.name: poetry.installation.chooser.Chooser

methods.name: poetry.installation.chooser.Chooser.choose_for
documentation: Return the url of the selected archive for a given package.

methods.name: poetry.installation.chooser.Chooser._sort_key
documentation:
Function to pass as the `key` argument to a call to sorted() to sort
InstallationCandidates by preference.
Returns a tuple such that tuples sorting as greater using Python's
default comparison operator are more preferred.
The preference is as follows:
First and foremost, candidates with allowed (matching) hashes are
always preferred over candidates without matching hashes. This is
because e.g. if the only candidate with an allowed hash is yanked,
we still want to use that candidate.
Second, excepting hash considerations, candidates that have been
yanked (in the sense of PEP 592) are always less preferred than
candidates that haven't been yanked. Then:
If not finding wheels, they are sorted by version only.
If finding wheels, then the sort order is by version, then:
  1. existing installs
  2. wheels ordered via Wheel.support_index_min(self._supported_tags)
  3. source archives
If prefer_binary was set, then all wheels are sorted above sources.
Note: it was considered to embed this logic into the Link
      comparison operators, but then different sdist links
      with the same version, would have to be considered equal
A Chooser chooses an appropriate release archive for packages.

classes.name: poetry.factory.Factory
documentation: Factory class to create various elements needed by Poetry.

classes.name: poetry.mixology.incompatibility_cause.IncompatibilityCause
documentation: The reason and Incompatibility's terms are incompatible.

classes.name: poetry.mixology.incompatibility_cause.ConflictCause

The incompatibility was derived from two existing incompatibilities
during conflict resolution.

classes.name: poetry.mixology.incompatibility_cause.PythonCause
documentation:
The incompatibility represents a package's python constraint
(Python versions) being incompatible
with the current python version.

classes.name: poetry.mixology.incompatibility_cause.PlatformCause
documentation:
The incompatibility represents a package's platform constraint
(OS most likely) being incompatible with the current platform.

classes.name: poetry.mixology.incompatibility_cause.PackageNotFoundCause
documentation:
The incompatibility represents a package that couldn't be found by its
source.

functions.name: poetry.inspection.info.get_pep517_metadata
documentation:
Helper method to use PEP-517 library to build and read package metadata.

:param path: Path to package source to build and read metadata for.

methods.name: poetry.inspection.info.PackageInfo.asdict
documentation: Helper method to convert package info into a dictionary used for caching.

methods.name: poetry.inspection.info.PackageInfo.load
documentation:
Helper method to load data from a dictionary produced by `PackageInfo.asdict()`.

:param data: Data to load. This is expected to be a `dict` object output by
    `asdict()`.

methods.name: poetry.inspection.info.PackageInfo.to_package
documentation:
Create a new `poetry.core.packages.package.Package` instance using metadata from
this instance.

:param name: Name to use for the package, if not specified name from this
    instance is used.
:param extras: Extras to activate for this package.
:param root_dir:  Optional root directory to use for the package. If set,
    dependency strings will be parsed relative to this directory.

methods.name: poetry.inspection.info.PackageInfo._from_distribution
documentation:
Helper method to parse package information from a `pkginfo.Distribution`
instance.

:param dist: The distribution instance to parse information from.

methods.name: poetry.inspection.info.PackageInfo._from_sdist_file
documentation:
Helper method to parse package information from an sdist file. We attempt to
first inspect the file using `pkginfo.SDist`. If this does not provide us with
package requirements, we extract the source and handle it as a directory.

:param path: The sdist file to parse information from.

methods.name: poetry.inspection.info.PackageInfo.from_setup_files
documentation:
Mechanism to parse package information from a `setup.[py|cfg]` file. This uses
the implementation at `poetry.utils.setup_reader.SetupReader` in order to parse
the file. This is not reliable for complex setup files and should only attempted
as a fallback.

:param path: Path to `setup.py` file

methods.name: poetry.inspection.info.PackageInfo._find_dist_info
documentation:
Discover all `*.*-info` directories in a given path.

:param path: Path to search.

methods.name: poetry.inspection.info.PackageInfo.from_metadata
documentation:
Helper method to parse package information from an unpacked metadata directory.

:param path: The metadata directory to parse information from.

methods.name: poetry.inspection.info.PackageInfo.from_package
documentation:
Helper method to inspect a `Package` object, in order to generate package info.

:param package: This must be a poetry package instance.

methods.name: poetry.inspection.info.PackageInfo.from_directory
documentation:
Generate package information from a package source directory. If `disable_build`
is not `True` and introspection of all available metadata fails, the package is
attempted to be built in an isolated environment so as to generate required
metadata.

:param path: Path to generate package information from.
:param disable_build: If not `True` and setup reader fails, PEP 517 isolated
    build is attempted in order to gather metadata.

methods.name: poetry.inspection.info.PackageInfo.from_sdist
documentation:
Gather package information from an sdist file, packed or unpacked.

:param path: Path to an sdist file or unpacked directory.

methods.name: poetry.inspection.info.PackageInfo.from_wheel
documentation:
Gather package information from a wheel.

:param path: Path to wheel.

methods.name: poetry.inspection.info.PackageInfo.from_bdist
documentation:
Gather package information from a bdist (wheel etc.).

:param path: Path to bdist.

methods.name: poetry.inspection.info.PackageInfo.from_path
documentation:
Gather package information from a given path (bdist, sdist, directory).

:param path: Path to inspect.

classes.name: poetry.mixology.partial_solution.PartialSolution

documentation:
Adds an assignment of package as a decision
and increments the decision level.

methods.name: poetry.mixology.partial_solution.PartialSolution.derive
documentation: Adds an assignment of package as a derivation.

methods.name: poetry.mixology.partial_solution.PartialSolution._assign
documentation: Adds an Assignment to _assignments and _positive or _negative.

methods.name: poetry.mixology.partial_solution.PartialSolution.backtrack
documentation:
Resets the current decision level to decision_level, and removes all
assignments made after that level.

methods.name: poetry.mixology.partial_solution.PartialSolution._register
documentation: Registers an Assignment in _positive or _negative.

methods.name: poetry.mixology.partial_solution.PartialSolution.satisfier
documentation:
Returns the first Assignment in this solution such that the sublist of
assignments up to and including that entry collectively satisfies term.

# A list of Assignments that represent the solver's current best guess about
# what's true for the eventual set of package versions that will comprise the
# total solution.
#
# See:
# https://github.com/dart-lang/mixology/tree/master/doc/solver.md#partial-solution.

classes.name: poetry.plugins.base_plugin.BasePlugin

The `activate()` method must be implemented and receives the Poetry instance.

classes.name: poetry.mixology.term.Term

methods.name: poetry.mixology.term.Term.satisfies
documentation: Returns whether this term satisfies another.

methods.name: poetry.mixology.term.Term._relation
documentation:
Returns the relationship between the package versions
allowed by this term and another.

methods.name: poetry.mixology.term.Term._intersect
documentation:
Returns a Term that represents the packages
allowed by both this term and another

methods.name: poetry.mixology.term.Term.difference
documentation:
Returns a Term that represents packages
allowed by this term and not by the other

A statement about a package which is true or false for a given selection of
package versions.

See https://github.com/dart-lang/pub/tree/master/doc/solver.md#term.

classes.name: poetry.plugins.plugin_manager.PluginManager

methods.name: poetry.plugins.plugin_manager.PluginManager._is_plugin_candidate
documentation:
Helper method to check if given entry point is a valid as a plugin candidate.
When an environment is specified, the entry point's associated distribution
should be installed, and discoverable in the given environment.
This class registers and activates plugins.

classes.name: poetry.publishing.publisher.Publisher
documentation: Registers and publishes packages to remote repositories.

methods.name: poetry.installation.installer.Installer.lock
documentation: Prepare the installer for locking only.

methods.name: poetry.installation.installer.Installer._execute_operation

methods.name: poetry.installation.installer.Installer._get_extra_packages
documentation:
Returns all package names required by extras.

Maybe we just let the solver handle it?

methods.name: poetry.repositories.cached.CachedRepository.get_release_info
documentation:
Return the release information given a package name and a version.

The information is returned from the cache if it exists
or retrieved from the remote server.

classes.name: poetry.mixology.version_solver.DependencyCache
documentation:
A cache of the valid dependencies.

The key observation here is that during the search - except at backtracking
- once we have decided that a dependency is invalid, we never need check it
again.

classes.name: poetry.mixology.version_solver.VersionSolver

methods.name: poetry.mixology.version_solver.VersionSolver.solve
documentation:
Finds a set of dependencies that match the root package's constraints,
or raises an error if no such set is available.

methods.name: poetry.mixology.version_solver.VersionSolver._propagate
documentation:
Performs unit propagation on incompatibilities transitively
related to package to derive new assignments for _solution.

methods.name: poetry.mixology.version_solver.VersionSolver._propagate_incompatibility
documentation:
If incompatibility is almost satisfied by _solution, adds the
negation of the unsatisfied term to _solution.

If incompatibility is satisfied by _solution, returns _conflict. If
incompatibility is almost satisfied by _solution, returns the
unsatisfied term's package name.

Otherwise, returns None.

methods.name: poetry.mixology.version_solver.VersionSolver._resolve_conflict
documentation:
Given an incompatibility that's satisfied by \_solution,
The [conflict resolution] constructs a new incompatibility that encapsulates
the root cause of the conflict and backtracks \_solution until the new
incompatibility will allow \_propagate() to deduce new assignments.

Adds the new incompatibility to \_incompatibilities and returns it.

(conflict-resolution)=

<https://github.com/dart-lang/pub/tree/master/doc/solver.md#conflict-resolution>

methods.name: poetry.mixology.version_solver.VersionSolver._choose_package_version
documentation:
Tries to select a version of a required package.

Returns the name of the package whose incompatibilities should be
propagated by _propagate(), or None indicating that version solving is
complete and a solution has been found.

methods.name: poetry.mixology.version_solver.VersionSolver._result
documentation: Creates a #SolverResult from the decisions in _solution

The version solver that finds a set of package versions that satisfy the
root package's dependencies.

See https://github.com/dart-lang/pub/tree/master/doc/solver.md for details
on how this solver works.

classes.name: poetry.repositories.lockfile_repository.LockfileRepository

Special repository that distinguishes packages not only by name and version,

methods.name: poetry.mixology.incompatibility.Incompatibility.external_incompatibilities

Returns all external incompatibilities in this incompatibility's

methods.name: poetry.repositories.single_page_repository.SinglePageRepository._get_page

methods.name: poetry.repositories.link_sources.base.LinkSource.clean_link
documentation:
Makes sure a link is fully encoded.  That is, if a ' ' shows up in
the link, it will be rewritten to %20 (while not over-quoting
% or other characters).

methods.name: poetry.repositories.legacy_repository.LegacyRepository.package
documentation:
Retrieve the release information.

This is a heavy task which takes time.
We have to download a package to get the dependencies.
We also need to download every file matching this release
to get the various hashes.

Note that this will be cached so the subsequent operations
should be much faster.

methods.name: poetry.repositories.legacy_repository.LegacyRepository._find_packages
documentation: Find packages on the remote server.

methods.name: poetry.packages.locker.Locker.locked_repository

methods.name: poetry.packages.locker.Locker._get_content_hash
documentation: Returns the sha256 hash of the sorted content of the pyproject file.

methods.name: poetry.publishing.uploader.Uploader._register
documentation: Register a package to a repository.

methods.name: poetry.repositories.installed_repository.InstalledRepository.get_package_paths
documentation:
Process a .pth file within the site-packages directories, and return any valid
paths. We skip executable .pth files as there is no reliable means to do this
without side-effects to current run-time. Mo check is made that the item refers
to a directory rather than a file, however, in order to maintain backwards
compatibility, we allow non-existing paths to be discovered. The latter
behaviour is different to how Python's site-specific hook configuration works.

Reference: https://docs.python.org/3.8/library/site.html

:param env: The environment to search for the .pth file in.
:param name: The name of the package to search .pth file for.
:return: A `Set` of valid `Path` objects.

methods.name: poetry.repositories.installed_repository.InstalledRepository.load
documentation: Load installed packages.

functions.name: poetry.utils.extras.get_extra_package_names
documentation:
Returns all package names required by the given extras.

:param packages: A collection of packages, such as from Repository.packages
:param extras: A mapping of `extras` names to lists of package names, as defined
    in the `extras` section of `poetry.lock`.
:param extra_names: A list of strings specifying names of extra groups to resolve.

Given a package name and optional version,
returns the latest Package that matches

classes.name: poetry.utils.shell.Shell

Represents the current shell.

functions.name: poetry.utils.helpers.remove_directory
documentation:
Helper function handle safe removal, and optionally forces stubborn file removal.
This is particularly useful when dist files are read-only or git writes read-only
files on Windows.

Internally, all arguments are passed to `shutil.rmtree`.

methods.name: poetry.utils.password_manager.PasswordManager.get_pypi_token
documentation:
Get PyPi token.

First checks the environment variables for a token,
then the configured username/password and the
available keyring.

:param repo_name:  Name of repository.
:return: Returns a token as a string if found, otherwise None.

classes.name: poetry.utils.setup_reader.SetupReader
documentation: Class that reads a setup.py file without executing it.

methods.name: poetry.vcs.git.backend.GitRefSpec.resolve
documentation: Resolve the ref using the provided remote refs.

methods.name: poetry.vcs.git.backend.GitRefSpec._normalise
documentation:
Internal helper method to determine if given revision is
    1. a branch or tag; if so, set corresponding properties.
    2. a short sha; if so, resolve full sha and set as revision

methods.name: poetry.vcs.git.backend.GitRefSpec._set_head
documentation:
Internal helper method to populate ref and set it's sha as the remote's head
and default ref.

methods.name: poetry.vcs.git.backend.Git._fetch_remote_refs
documentation: Helper method to fetch remote refs.

methods.name: poetry.vcs.git.backend.Git._clone_legacy
documentation:
Helper method to facilitate fallback to using system provided git client via
subprocess calls.

methods.name: poetry.vcs.git.backend.Git._clone
documentation:
Helper method to clone a remove repository at the given `url` at the specified
ref spec.

methods.name: poetry.vcs.git.backend.Git._clone_submodules
documentation: Helper method to identify configured submodules and clone them recursively.

methods.name: poetry.puzzle.provider.Provider.search_for_installed_packages
documentation:
Search for installed packages, when available, that satisfy the given
dependency.

This is useful when dealing with packages that are under development, not
published on package sources and/or only available via system installations.

methods.name: poetry.puzzle.provider.Provider.search_for
documentation:
Search for the specifications that match the given dependency.

The specifications in the returned list will be considered in reverse
order, so the latest version ought to be last.

methods.name: poetry.puzzle.provider.Provider._search_for_vcs
documentation:
Search for the specifications that match the given VCS dependency.

Basically, we clone the repository in a temporary directory
and get the information we need by checking out the specified reference.

methods.name: poetry.puzzle.provider.Provider.incompatibilities_for
documentation:
Returns incompatibilities that encapsulate a given package's dependencies,
or that it can't be safely selected.

If multiple subsequent versions of this package have the same
dependencies, this will return incompatibilities that reflect that. It
won't return incompatibilities that have already been returned by a
previous call to _incompatibilities_for().

functions.name: poetry.utils.env.build_environment
documentation:
If a build script is specified for the project, there could be additional build
time dependencies, eg: cython, setuptools etc. In these cases, we create an
ephemeral build environment with all requirements specified under
`build-system.requires` and return this. Otherwise, the given default project
environment is returned.

classes.name: poetry.utils.env.EnvManager

methods.name: poetry.utils.env.EnvManager.get_system_env
documentation:
Retrieve the current Python environment.

This can be the base Python environment or an activated virtual environment.

This method also workaround the issue that the virtual environment
used by Poetry internally (when installed via the custom installer)
is incorrectly detected as the system environment. Note that this workaround
happens only when `naive` is False since there are times where we actually
want to retrieve Poetry's custom virtual environment
(e.g. plugin installation or self update).
Environments manager

methods.name: poetry.utils.env.Env._run

methods.name: poetry.utils.env.Env._bin

classes.name: poetry.utils.env.SystemEnv

classes.name: poetry.utils.env.VirtualEnv
